title: Angolluma lugardii
intro: Angolluma lugardii, also known as the Angolluma or the Devil's Apple, is a species of succulent plant in the Apocynaceae family, native to the deserts of the Middle East and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, skin infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Angolluma lugardii include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbalism, Angolluma lugardii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or topical cream, and is also used in the form of ointments, salves, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
